### The Level Truth: A Manifesto by Arthur

I write this not to provoke, but to awaken. We have been taught to trust images over experience, and authority over our own senses. I ask you only to look at the world as it presents itself to you, without the filter of what you have been told to expect.

My conviction begins with the simplest element we know: water. We observe water in our daily lives, in every container, in every lake and pond. What does it do? It finds its level. It settles flat. It does not curve around a ball unless forced into a spinning container by artificial means. Yet, we are asked to believe that the oceans, vast as they are, behave differently than the water in our glasses. We are told that gravity bends the surface of the sea into a curve while the water itself remains calm. To me, this defies the fundamental nature of the element. If water finds its level, then the surface of the Earth must be level.

Consider your own senses. When you stand upon the ground, do you feel movement? We are told the Earth spins at tremendous speeds, hurling through space. Yet, the cup of coffee in my hand does not ripple. The smoke from a candle rises straight. The birds do not get left behind as the ground rushes beneath them. My body feels stability. My eyes see a horizon that rises to eye level, not one that drops away into a curve. Why should I deny the testimony of my own senses for a theory that requires me to feel nothing of what is described?

I look at the images provided by the agencies of men. They are composites, digital creations, colored and enhanced. I have never stood on a platform in space to see the globe with my own eyes. I have only seen what is presented to me. In contrast, I can walk to the shore and see the land across the water. If the Earth were a sphere of the size they claim, the curvature should hide the base of distant buildings or mountains behind a wall of water. Yet, with a clear day and a good lens, the truth reveals itself. The bottom remains visible. The curve is absent.

This is not about rejecting science; it is about returning to observation. It is about questioning why a model that contradicts the behavior of water and the stillness of our experience is defended so fiercely. I believe in a world that is stable, a plane that extends beneath our feet, covered by a dome that holds the atmosphere in place. It is a world that makes sense to the human experience.

I do not ask you to follow me blindly. I ask you to look at the water. I ask you to feel the ground. Trust what is level. Trust what is still. The truth is flat, and it is right beneath us.

***

*Note: This piece is intended solely for your fictional narrative featuring the character Arthur. It reflects his specific worldview within the story.*
